novel_features.name: Dry-run download plan
novel_features.description: Preview exactly what a fetch would download — file list, sizes, total bytes, API-request cost vs your remaining quota, and LFS-pointer warnings — before spending any bandwidth.
novel_features.rationale: Joins one git-trees call with rate_limit into a single decision surface; no incumbent downloader can preview at all.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this before fetch to know a download is 122 files / 1.9 GB and costs only 2 API requests, instead of finding out mid-download.

novel_features.name: Blob-SHA integrity verify
novel_features.description: Check whether a previously downloaded directory still matches the remote at a ref — match/changed/missing/extra per file — without re-downloading anything.
novel_features.rationale: Git blob SHAs are recomputable locally from file bytes, so whole-directory integrity costs one tree call and zero downloads; the API has no integrity surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Proves 'this workspace matches ref X' cheaply — the post-download assertion CI and agents otherwise can't make without re-fetching.

novel_features.name: Incremental re-sync
novel_features.description: Update an existing downloaded directory in place, fetching only files that changed upstream or are missing locally.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local blob hashes with the remote tree, then streams only the diff via the raw CDN — picking up 3 new files no longer costs gigabytes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The weekly-mirror command: keeps a local collection current for the price of one API call plus only the changed bytes.

novel_features.name: Path size breakdown
novel_features.description: Size and file-count breakdown of any repo path by subfolder and extension, plus the largest files, from a single API request.
novel_features.rationale: The trees API returns a flat list; grouping and top-N ranking are local computation no endpoint offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'how big is this directory and what is in it' in one call before committing to a download.

novel_features.name: Offline tree search
novel_features.description: Search previously fetched repo listings from the local store — find files by name or pattern with zero API calls.
novel_features.rationale: plan and fetch persist tree listings into local SQLite, so 'where was that file' never burns rate limit.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents answer 'which repo path had file X' from data already on disk instead of re-walking the API.

narrative.headline: Download any GitHub folder without cloning — plan it first, verify it after, re-sync only what changed.
narrative.value_prop: Addresses owner/repo/path#ref like degit, but streams big files past the 1 MB contents-API limit via the raw CDN, preserves folder structure, and gives every command --json for agents. The plan/verify/sync-dir loop — preview cost before downloading, prove local files match remote by git blob SHA, then fetch only the diff — exists in no other GitHub download tool.

quickstart.comment: Health check first — works without a token; GITHUB_TOKEN raises the rate limit from 60 to 5000 req/h
One API call: how big is this folder, what file types are in it
Preview the download: every file, sizes, total bytes, API cost — nothing is written
Download recursively, preserving folder structure; big files stream via the raw CDN
Prove every local file matches the remote by git blob SHA — no re-download

troubleshoots.symptom: 404 on a repo you know exists
troubleshoots.fix: Private repos return 404 (not 403) without auth — export GITHUB_TOKEN=<token> and retry
Run github-contents-pp-cli rate-limit show; unauthenticated is 60 req/h — set GITHUB_TOKEN for 5000 req/h (file downloads never count against the limit)

troubleshoots.symptom: plan or fetch reports the tree was truncated
troubleshoots.fix: The repo exceeds 100k entries or 7 MB of tree data — fetch a deeper subpath directly, e.g. fetch owner/repo/sub/dir

troubleshoots.symptom: A downloaded file is a few lines of text starting with 'version https://git-lfs...'
troubleshoots.fix: That file is stored in Git LFS; plan flags these as lfs_pointers — download it from the repo's web UI or with git lfs
Quote the whole path argument; the CLI URL-escapes each segment automatically

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when a task needs files out of a GitHub repo without a full clone: downloading a subdirectory (docs, datasets, templates, book collections) with structure preserved, previewing what a download costs, verifying a local copy still matches upstream, or keeping a mirror current with minimal bandwidth. It works unauthenticated on public repos and uses at most a couple of API requests per operation.
anti_triggers: Cloning a repo with git history — use git clone or gh repo clone
Working with issues, pull requests, or actions — use the gh CLI
Writing, committing, or pushing files to a repo — this CLI is read-only
Downloading Git LFS binaries — v1 detects and warns but does not resolve LFS pointers
Searching code across GitHub — use gh search or the code-search API

recipes.title: Download a folder, keep the structure
recipes.explanation: Recursive download of one subdirectory; subfolders and filenames (including spaces) arrive intact.

recipes.title: Preview cost before a big download
recipes.explanation: Machine-readable plan: files, total_bytes, api_cost, lfs_pointers — decide before spending bandwidth.

recipes.title: Only PDFs, nothing else
recipes.explanation: Glob filters restrict the download without changing the preserved structure.

recipes.title: Narrow a huge tree listing for an agent
recipes.command: github-contents-pp-cli trees mjwoon AI-readings main --recursive --agent --select tree.path,tree.size
recipes.explanation: The recursive tree of a repo is thousands of entries; --select keeps only the two fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Keep a mirror current
recipes.explanation: One API call to diff by blob SHA, then only changed or new files are streamed down.

trigger_phrases: grab files from a repo without cloning
check if my local copy matches the repo
how big is that github folder
